summary: Phase IV SYNAPTIQ Messaging tested end-to-end. Backend pytest: 24/25 passing. Coverage: Phase I-III regression (discover/feed, journals, billing/plans, credits/balance all 200), direct conversation idempotency, can't-message-self → 400, third-party gets different conv id, collaboration context get-or-create + idempotent, list/filter by type, search by q=Marcus, message POST returns sender+attachments[]+shared_resources[]+mentions, list ordered oldest→newest, non-member 403 on GET and POST, read receipts drop unread to 0 and unread/count endpoint works, mention notification created for Marcus (type='mention'), uploads PDF (kind=file) and PNG (kind=image), 415 on text/plain, attachment round-trip through message, Marcus (member) can download attachment 200 but Aiko (non-member) gets 403, shared_resources of type 'journal' accepted and 'video' silently filtered (length=1), WebSocket cookie auth: member receives broadcast on new message, non-member rejected with 4403, no-cookie rejected with 4401. Frontend: Elena → /messages renders all 6 filter pills (All/Direct/Collab/Project/Workspace/Manuscript), search input, 3 conv items with unread badge (1) on Marcus direct from earlier WS test message. Clicking conv → /messages/c/{id}. Thread shows composer with all 5 testids (message-list, message-input, message-send-btn, msg-attach-btn, msg-share-btn). Send posts and appears immediately. Share modal opens with all 6 tabs (journal/conference/grant/publication/project/manuscript) and Journal tab loads Nature/Science/Cell/Lancet/JAMA etc. Attachment chip (hello.pdf) renders inside the message bubble from the earlier backend round-trip test. Shared resource chip (Nature/Springer Nature) renders in message bubble. open-chat-btn present and functional on CollaborationDetail (→ /messages/c/{id}), ProjectDetail, ManuscriptDetail.

minor.issue: Returns 200 (silently creates a conv) instead of 400/404. Root cause: /app/backend/routers/messaging.py:_resolve_context_members (lines 199-202) catches ObjectId() failure and returns ([fallback_user_id], capitalized type) instead of raising. Spec explicitly says invalid context_id must return 400/404.

action_items: Fix _resolve_context_members in /app/backend/routers/messaging.py to raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid context_id') when ObjectId(context_id) parsing fails — currently it silently falls back to a 200 with no membership beyond the creator.
critical_code_review_comments: messaging.py: get-or-create direct uses sorted user IDs for context_key (line 117-118) → guaranteed idempotency regardless of caller order. Solid.
messaging.py: context conv resync of memberships on every fetch (lines 160-170) ensures newly-accepted collab members appear in chat — good for race-with-acceptance flows.
messaging.py:_resolve_context_members swallows invalid ObjectId by returning fallback list (line 200-202) — this is the source of the only failing test; must raise 400 or 404.

messaging.py:download_file authorizes owner OR member of any conversation where the attachment is referenced — correct row-level security; the message lookup pattern db.messages.find_one({attachment_ids: attachment_id}) relies on attachment_ids being stored as plain strings (not ObjectId), which matches the insert path. Good.

messaging.py:WebSocket reads access_token directly from websocket.cookies and decodes with JWT_SECRET; rejects non-members with 4403 and missing/invalid tokens with 4401 (verified). The exclude_ws on broadcast for typing/presence prevents echoing to sender.
messaging.py:post_message marks the sender as read up to now (line 452-455) so they never see their own messages as unread. Correct.
storage_service.py: 403 → re-init key + retry pattern (lines 38-47, 55-59) handles session expiry. Init is idempotent via cached _storage_key. Good.

ConnectionManager (realtime.py) is in-process only; broadcasts will not fan out across multiple uvicorn workers if scaling horizontally — acceptable for current single-worker deployment but worth noting for prod scale.

MENTION_RE handles @local-part by matching against email local-part lower-case (line 375-380); also tries dotted-fullname collapse. Could miss '@Marcus Okafor' style but spec accepts simple handles only.

Frontend Messages.jsx exposes all required testids via /lib/testIds.js (convList, convSearch, convItem(id), convFilter(kind), convUnread(id), messageInput, messageSendBtn, messageList, attachBtn, shareBtn, shareTab(kind), typingIndicator, openChatBtn) — testability strong.

CollaborationDetail/ProjectDetail/WorkspaceDetail/ManuscriptDetail all wire openChatBtn → navigate('/messages', { state: { openContext: { type, id } } }) and Messages page consumes the state to POST /api/conversations and redirect to /messages/c/{id} (verified for Collab, Project, Manuscript).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_phase4_test.py (new — 25 tests covering conversations, messages, attachments, shared resources, WS)
test_credentials: elena.varga@synaptiq.io / demo123 (member of seeded collab), marcus.okafor@synaptiq.io / demo123 (direct + collab member), aiko.tanaka@synaptiq.io / demo123 (non-member for 403 tests)
seed_data_creation: Tests create a direct conv (Elena↔Marcus), a direct conv (Marcus↔Aiko), a collaboration-context conv, several messages with content/mentions/attachments/shared_resources, two uploaded attachments (one PDF reused for message round-trip, one PNG). No teardown — data is namespaced and idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_phase4_test.py at /app/backend/tests/. Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/backend_phase4_test.py -v --asyncio-mode=auto. Requires websockets + pytest-asyncio (installed). The only failing test is TestContextConversations::test_invalid_context, due to the documented backend bug in _resolve_context_members. Once main agent fixes that to raise 400 for non-ObjectId context_id, the suite will be 25/25. Phase III suite (backend_phase3_test.py) still passes. Note: collaboration conv id resolution uses the FIRST collab returned by /api/collaborations as Elena — if seed changes, the test will pick a different collab automatically.
rca of the issue: test_invalid_context (single failure). Reproduction: POST /api/conversations {type:'project', context_id:'invalidhex'} as Elena → 200 with new conv id. Expected: 400 (or 404). Root cause: /app/backend/routers/messaging.py lines 198-202 — _resolve_context_members wraps ObjectId(context_id) in try/except and on failure returns ([fallback_user_id], capitalized_type) instead of raising HTTPException. Then _get_or_create_context line 173 happily inserts a conversation with context_id='invalidhex' and only the creator as member. Mitigation: replace lines 200-202 with `raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Invalid context_id')`. This is consistent with how the rest of the function raises 404 when the resolved ObjectId is not found in the corresponding collection.
